entry.title: What's New in the ODN Framework for S/4HANA Cloud 2608?
entry.content:
Scope

Scope Note: This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 and later. Two enhancements to the ODN Framework extend automatic Official Document Number handling into reversal postings and VF02 billing edits — both are customer-managed configurations. No SAP Note or custom code required.

The ODN Framework keeps evolving.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, two of the most-requested scenarios — FI reversals and VF02 billing edits — are now handled by configuration alone.

Reversals in FI — Three Options, Zero Custom Code

FI reversal postings now integrate end-to-end with the ODN Framework. When a customer reverses a document, the framework decides how the reversal document is numbered based on the configuration. You can choose the behavior that fits each country and process:

  • Copy the ODN from the original document — common in many EU markets where the reversal must reference the same official number as the original.
  • Assign a brand-new ODN — based on your framework configuration, treating the reversal as a fresh officially numbered document.
  • Leave the reversal without an ODN — no action taken, useful where reversals are internal-only and never carry statutory numbering.

This closes one of the most-requested gaps for countries where reversals carry strict numbering rules and removes the need for custom coding to bridge original and reversal documents.

VF02 Billing Edits — Automatic FI Sync

When someone edits a billing document in transaction VF02, the corresponding FI document's ODN entry is now updated automatically. The framework enforces two clear rules:

  • If the FI document's ODN field was empty, it is filled from the billing document.
  • If the FI document already had an ODN, consistency is enforced and any divergence is prevented.

The result: no more silent divergence between Sales and Finance, no more manual reconciliation, and no more support tickets asking “why doesn't the number match?”

Practical Takeaway

To take advantage of these enhancements:

  • Review the ODN configuration for reversal scenarios in your affected company codes.
  • Decide per country which of the three reversal behaviors applies (copy, new, or none).
  • Retire any custom code you have in place today to synchronize ODNs between billing (SD) and FI documents — the framework now does it for you.
  • Test with one reversal and one VF02 edit in each country to confirm the outcome matches expectations.

Summary

The ODN Framework in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 covers more of the real-world document lifecycle out of the box — especially reversals and VF02 edits — while making the framework easier to consume through clearer, more descriptive configuration. Fewer country-specific one-offs, more reuse, and a cleaner path toward end-to-end localization delivered as a self-service capability.

Key Takeaways

  • Reversals covered end-to-end — choose per country whether to copy the original ODN, assign a new one, or leave the reversal without an ODN.
  • Sales-to-Finance consistency — a VF02 billing edit automatically updates the FI document's ODN entry, eliminating manual reconciliation.
  • No custom code needed — both scenarios are handled by standard configuration in the ODN Framework, replacing the country-specific enhancements many customers used to rely on.
  • Continuous framework evolution — SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 is another step toward the ODN Framework as your single, harmonized numbering backbone across Finance and Sales.

More Information

  • Official Document Numbering for Reversed Invoices in Financial Accounting | SAP Help Portal
  • Customer Invoices in Sales | SAP Help Portal

Published by SAP Globalization Services · Feedback and questions welcome in the comments below.

entry.title: Index Linked Bond - Security valuation - Book value

How to exclude Security valuations from the book value of TPM1 valuations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Index Linked Bond - Security valuation - Book value
entry.content: When executing TPM1, use the valuation category "Mid-Year Valuation with Reset" for the Security Valuation step. This category posts the security valuation for the current period but resets/reverses it at the start of the next period, ensuring the next TPM1 run does not carry forward the security valuation delta as part of the base.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow Delta Run Fails: “Spa...
entry.content:
Hi, 

you can use the same source in two different spaces, but you cannot use the same destination. This is the issue. You have to create two target Azure Tables and merge them via union. 

3464320 - Error "space tables exist in other spaces" when deploying a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere - SAP for Me

The problem is locking of the target table. 

Martin

entry.title: What does the Reltio Acquisition Mean for Master Data Management at SAP?
entry.content:
Master data is the foundation of every trusted business process, analytical insight, and AI use case. With the introduction of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) and the recent acquisition of Reltio, SAP customers now have a more powerful set of choices for how to govern, unify, and trust their master data. Since the SAP Sapphire announcement, one question keeps surfacing from customers and partners alike: When should we choose SAP Master Data Governance (MDG), when should we choose Reltio, and when do we need both?

And if you’re a long-standing SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) customer - like the enterprises that have invested in customizing SAP MDG to fit their global master data management (MDM) and governance processes - SAP’s Reltio acquisition likely triggered a mix of curiosity and concern:

  • Does Reltio replace SAP MDG?
  • What happens to our years of MDG customization and process integration?
  • Where does Reltio fit in with our data, analytics, and AI landscape?
  • How and when do we get started?

The short answer: your SAP MDG investment is preserved, modernized, and extended. Reltio will be a complementary MDM capability inside SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) with its own set of strengths based on the customer’s desired outcome.

Why Master Data Management (MDM) Matters More Than Ever

SAP’s strategic direction - the Autonomous Enterprise - depends on a strong foundation: the SAP Business AI Platform, which combines deep process context, unified data, and purpose-built models with enterprise governance, so you can build and integrate AI that understands your business, not just your prompts. A core component of that platform is SAP Business Data Cloud, the data foundation that unifies, governs, and delivers AI-ready data across SAP and third-party sources. 

  1. Foundation: Unified Lake Storage
    At the foundation, lake storage unifies SAP and non-SAP data across multi-cloud, hybrid, and on-premises environments. This layer remains essential and enduring.
  2. Intelligent Compute
    Above the foundation, data is processed through intelligent compute, powering data lakehouses, warehouses, machine learning, and data engineering workloads-the areas where most innovation has occurred over the past decade.
  3. The Knowledge Core (What Changes Everything)
    What’s new and truly transformative is above the compute layer. The knowledge core introduces a paradigm where data is no longer merely stored and processed but understood in the context of how the business operates.

This is why metadata is foundational to the entire system. And not just technical metadata like schemas or pipelines, but active, business-aware metadata that carries meaningful context, including definitions of metrics and KPIs, relationships between data entities, business processes and rules, lineage, usage, and quality signals.

This metadata acts as connective tissue, embedding business context directly into the data so it can be consistently understood and acted upon across the entire organization.

  1. Agents and Applications
    This contextual understanding then powers the top layer: agents and applications capable of self-learning and making autonomous decisions.

The true power of this architecture lies in how value compounds with each layer:

  • Intelligent compute delivers speed and scale
  • The knowledge core provides trust and business understanding
  • Agents deliver autonomous action grounded in that understanding

Together, these layers form a continuous cycle rather than a linear pipeline. High quality data informs context, context shapes decisions, and those decisions generate new data that continuously refines and improves the system.

Within this architecture, master data management is being introduced as a part of SAP BDC’s Knowledge Core, delivered through SAP MDG and Reltio as complementary components - both producing unified data products (what MDM experts have historically called the “golden record” or, in Reltio’s terminology, the “trusted record”).

SAP MDG: Central Governance at the Source

SAP Master Data Governance is about getting master data right from the start. It enforces governance within business processes at the point of creation, typically SAP Cloud ERP. Key strengths include:

  • Create trusted, governed master data at the source 
  • Enforce compliance and standards in every process 
  • Distribute directly to applications and to SAP BDC
  • Provide clean master data for all downstream consumption 
  • Ensure full transparency and auditability of changes 

The value promise: Data quality governed at the point of entry.

SAP MDG is now available as a commercial offering inside SAP BDC and delivered as SAP MDG private cloud, extra stack. Bi-directional integration between SAP MDG and the SAP S/4HANA remains a cornerstone of its value.

Reltio: Data Unification Across Any Source

Where SAP MDG governs at the source, Reltio unifies master data across systems including non-SAP applications and unstructured data through cloud-native, real-time integration. Reltio brings two complementary capabilities into the BDC portfolio:

  1. Data Unification
  • Unify master data across all systems into one view 
  • Resolve duplicates and create a consistent, trusted record 
  • Enable 360° visibility across domains (customers, suppliers, products), including unstructured sources 
  • Deliver unified data for any application or data product, with real-time operational access 

The value promise: High-quality trusted records for analytics and AI.

  1. Data Quality & Profiling
  • Assess and monitor data quality in disparate sources 
  • Make data issues visible across domains and systems 
  • Prioritize remediation based on business impact 
  • Continuously improve data quality at scale 

The value promise: Trusted data through ongoing monitoring and remediation.

Reltio is the unique provider enabling the SAP MDM portfolio in BDC to produce unified data products, reflecting a single source of truth.

Better Together: A Complementary Architecture

The vision SAP is executing toward is a single layer of master data management inside SAP BDC, where customers consume MDM as capabilities - spending SAP BDC capacity units - without needing to worry about which underlying component (SAP MDG or Reltio) is doing the work.

Here’s how the complementary architecture works today:

  • Producers (bottom): SAP Cloud ERP, SAP Ariba, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Commerce Cloud, and non-SAP applications feed data into BDC. 
  • Master Data Management in BDC (middle): SAP MDG (central governance at the point of entry in SAP Cloud ERP) and Reltio (cloud-native unification from any source) sit side by side, both producing high-quality master data that contribute towards trusted, unified data products.
  • Consumers (top): Joule agents, SAP CAP applications, SAP Analytics via SAP Datasphere, and SAP & non-SAP business applications consuming data in real time - including AI workloads grounded in trusted data. 

It’s a Choice of Data Strategy

The choice between SAP MDG and Reltio isn’t really an either/or - it’s an architecture driven decision based on an organization’s governance model, source landscape, and desired outcomes:

  • Use SAP MDG when governance and process control at the SAP application source is the priority. 
  • Use Reltio when unification, 360° views, and cross-system data quality - including non-SAP sources - are the priority. 
  • Use both when the desire is governed creation and enterprise-wide unification, which is increasingly the norm for AI-ready enterprises. 

For heavy SAP MDG adopters:

  • Your investment in SAP MDG is preserved and expanded as SAP MDG becomes a SAP BDC component. 
  • Reltio does not replace SAP MDG. Instead, it extends your MDM footprint beyond the SAP application landscape into non-SAP systems, unstructured data, and 360° domain views. 
  • You can begin adopting Reltio for use cases such as cloud-native unification, cross-system data quality, and 360° profile capabilities needed for a modern, AI-ready enterprise.

Final Thoughts

SAP’s long-term vision is clear: one coherent MDM layer inside SAP BDC, delivering high-quality unified data products that fuel analytics, operations, and AI.

For existing SAP MDG customers, the Reltio acquisition isn’t a disruption - it’s an expansion of what SAP MDM can do for you. SAP MDG today is the standard for governing master data at the SAP application source. Reltio brings the cloud-native unification, cross-system data quality, and 360° profile capabilities needed for a modern, AI-ready enterprise.

The most successful MDM customers will treat this not as a product decision, but as an architecture decision - using each solution where it plays to its strengths, and consuming both as capabilities inside SAP Business Data Cloud.

entry.title: PBXX condition type via custom logic during purchase order
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm working on a custom business requirement related to Purchase Order creation in SAP MM.

Requirement

During PO creation, the system should fetch the price from the custom condition record (KONP) based on the Material + Plant combination and populate NETPR.

Additionally:

  • If the user manually changes the NETPR to a value less than or equal to the condition price, it should be allowed.
  • If the user enters a value greater than the condition price, the system should overwrite it with the condition price.

Current Issue: Currently, SAP is populating NETPR with the price from the most recently created Purchase Order (PBXX condition), instead of the price from the condition record.

My Implementation: I have implemented the logic in the PO BAdI class (ZCL_*PROCESS_ITEM) to fetch the condition price from KONP and update POITEM-NETPR.

The logic works during the initial creation. However, whenever the user changes another field (such as Quantity or Delivery Date), the PROCESS_ITEM method is triggered again. At that point, SAP recalculates the pricing and updates NETPR with the last Purchase Order price (PBXX) before my logic can retain the intended value.

Any suggestions or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Allocation based on planned hours – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Customer Projects
entry.content:
Dear Sap Team,

I would really appreciate it if you could give me your opinion—based on your actual experience—on a situation we're facing.

What we’re working with

We’re on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, running Customer Project Management.

• We have over 2,500 commercial projects, adding up to roughly 8,000 WBS elements

• One important constraint: we’ve fully retired timesheets, so no actual hours are available to drive our allocation

• Our clear business need: allocate real costs from our central delivery cost centers down to WBS level, strictly based on the planned effort/hours that project managers maintain directly in each project

• We want to keep it 100% standard — using Universal Allocation only, no custom code, and no manual line‑by‑line work given the volume

What we'd like to hear your thoughts on:

• The straight forward “Planned Hours” option isn’t visible to us. Is it fully valid and supported to use Planned Values as our base type, reading from the plan ledger, to get exactly the same result — allocation driven by planned effort?

• Alternatively, would you consider bulk‑uploaded Statistical Key Figures the safest, most transparent way to make sure the driver is explicitly the planned hours we see in the projects?

• For roughly 8,000 WBS receivers, is a mass‑import approach stable and performant in your experience?

• Is there a simple configuration switch we might be missing that would expose planned effort/hours as a direct allocation base?

Any experience, tip or confirmation would be a huge help — we want to be confident we’re on the cleanest, most supported path.

Many thanks in advance,

entry.title: Re: Can we do Digital Signature For Multiple Billing Docu...
entry.content:
Hello @Ashish13 

 

Thank you for your question

 

In Public cloud, the BAdI EI_BADI_SIPT_DIGITAL_SIGN_SD is not the correct extensibility option. Therefore, I suggest not being used as the basis for a public cloud solution or a VF02-based mass process. The released option is APOC_SIGN_PDF_WITH_CERTFICATE, which digitally signs the PDF generated by the email output process using a certificate and an external signing service. This BAdI is available in Custom Logic through ABAP cloud extensibility.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9b351d6e33a0496598e4b58782565e8a/14c52b8663344d40b18d8d5424d2259a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/1d1ce0f3e61d4aac8be090d66816e699.html?locale=en-US

 

The important point is that this BAdI is executed per output item or PDF. It does not provide an interactive function where you select several billing documents in VF02 and signs them together in one question.

Here are steps you can consider:

You can configure digital signing as part of automatic billing output with the following steps:

You can implement APOC_SIGN_PDF_WITH_CERTIFICATE in Custom logic

Make sure you connect the implementation to the approved external certificate or signing service.

Configure the billing output type, channel EMAIL, recipient, form template, and dispatch timing in Output Parameter Determination.

Ensure you create or post the billing documents normally.

You need to allow Output Management generate and sign each invoice PDF automatically.

You can use Schedule Bulling Output when multiple billing documents must be processed in the background.

Cloud output control supports automatic creation and processing of output for customer invoices, including email output and background scheduling.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/d9cc17d1aa404aee9bf2d10599c9a8d1.html?locale=en-US

 

You carry out a test by creating two billing documents and process their email outputs. In the output monitoring screen, make sure you verify that each document has a separate output item with status Successfully Processed. Download both PDFs and ensure you confirm that the certificate information and digital signature validation are present.

If your requirement is one user action that signs several already generated PDFs together, I am not aware of any verified released standard app or BAdI for that mass interactive scenario. A side-by-side application or an external document signing platform would need to retrieve the billings PDF through a released interface, sign each document individually and manage the signed copies according to the applicable legal and archiving requirements. The Billing Document API supports retrieving billing documents PDFs but does not update them with a new signature inside the standard billing document.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/fe6f3eda2f914d81b512493fd79e9769.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have any further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Need Help: How to Remove Line Breaks (\r\n) in DocumentItemText within CDS View in ADT
entry.content: Hello everyone, I am developing a CDS View using ADT. I extract the field DocumentItemText, and I found there are line break characters inside this text field. I tried to use the replace() function to eliminate line breaks with the logic: replace( DocumentItemText, '\r\n', '' ) However, this approach does not work as expected. The line breaks still remain in the result. Could anyone advise the correct way to remove carriage return and line feed characters from text fields in CDS View? Any valid syntax or alternative function for stripping \r, \n inside CDS is highly appreciated. Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Restricting Users to View Only Their Own PR & PO
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are working on a business scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition where end users create Purchase Requisitions (PRs).

In our business process, the user who creates the Purchase Requisition is different from the purchasing user who processes the PR and creates the Purchase Order (PO).

Therefore, we would like to restrict end users so that they can only view the Purchase Requisitions they created and the related Purchase Orders created as a result of their own PRs.

For example:

  • User A → should only be able to view PRs created by User A.
  • User B → should only be able to view PRs created by User B.
  • User A should not be able to view User B's PRs.
  • Once the PR is approved and a related PO is created, User A should only be able to track the PO / purchasing process related to their own PR.
  • User A should not be able to view the POs or order tracking information created as a result of other users' approved PRs.
  • Purchasing users should still be able to view and process all PRs and POs within their assigned authorization scope.

Our main requirement is to prevent end users from accessing each other's purchasing process data. Therefore, we believe that the visibility of both Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders should be restricted based on the user who initiated the purchasing process.

We would like to understand the following:

  1. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, using standard authorization capabilities, to restrict a user so that they can only view Purchase Requisitions created by themselves?
  2. Is it possible to restrict Purchase Requisitions in Manage Purchase Requisitions or related PR applications based on the Requester / Created By user?
  3. Once a PR is approved and a PO is created, is it possible to restrict the user so that they can only view and track the PO related to their own PR?
  4. Is there a standard authorization or restriction available in My Purchase Orders or related purchase order tracking applications that allows users to view only the POs related to their own Purchase Requisitions?
  5. Is there a specific Restriction Type / Access Category or Business Role authorization that can be used to restrict both PR and PO visibility based on the Requester / Created By user?
  6. What is the standard SAP-recommended approach to allow purchasing users to access all relevant PRs and POs while restricting end users to only their own purchasing processes?
  7. Has anyone implemented a similar scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, could you please share the authorization design or solution approach you used?

We are specifically looking for a standard solution that prevents end users from viewing each other's Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders, while still allowing purchasing users to access and process all relevant documents within their authorization scope.

Thank you in advance for your help.

BR,
Evrim
@MerveBayturk @Melis_Sorgun @rodoplu_umut

entry.title: Why Doesn't My Asset Reach Zero Net Book Value at the End of Its Useful Life?
entry.content:
If you have ever reached the end of an asset's useful life and found a remaining Net Book Value (NBV) on the books — even though the asset should be fully depreciated — you are not alone. This is one of the most common sources of confusion in SAP Asset Accounting, and the good news is: the system is not wrong. There is a logical reason behind this behavior.

This post explains why it happens, walks through a concrete example with numbers, and outlines your options to address it.

The Setup: Depreciation from APC and Useful Life

The scenario described here applies when your depreciation key is configured to calculate depreciation based on APC (Acquisition and Production Costs) and useful life. This is a very common and straightforward setup:

Annual Depreciation = APC ÷ Useful Life

With this method, each acquisition amount is depreciated in equal instalments over the full useful life of the asset, starting from the ordinary depreciation start date of that specific acquisition.

That last part is the key.

Step 1: The Simple Case — One Acquisition, No Surprises

Let us start with a clean example. You capitalise an asset with an APC of 3,000 and a useful life of 3 years.

Year APC Used Depreciation Cumulative Dep. NBV

Y13,0001,0001,0002,000
Y23,0001,0002,0001,000
Y33,0001,0003,0000

Simple. At the end of year 3, the NBV is exactly zero. The asset is fully depreciated.

Step 2: A Subsequent Acquisition — Where the Residual Appears

Now suppose that in Year 2, you post an additional acquisition of 3,000 to the same asset. The useful life remains 3 years and you are not extending it.

The system now calculates depreciation independently for each acquisition posting, each starting from its own depreciation start date:

Original acquisition (starts Year 1):

Year Depreciation

Y13,000 ÷ 3 = 1,000
Y23,000 ÷ 3 = 1,000
Y33,000 ÷ 3 = 1,000
Total3,000 — fully depreciated ✓

Second acquisition (starts Year 2):

Year Depreciation

Y1— (not yet capitalised)
Y23,000 ÷ 3 = 1,000
Y33,000 ÷ 3 = 1,000
Total2,000 — only 2 of 3 years depreciated ✗

Combined asset view:

Year Total Depreciation Cumulative NBV

Y11,0001,0005,000
Y22,0003,0003,000
Y32,0005,0001,000

At the end of Year 3 (end of useful life), 1,000 remains on the asset. The system did not make a mistake. The second acquisition simply did not have enough time to be fully depreciated within the original useful life.

A Real Example from the System

The table below mirrors a real asset with an APC of 22,460.02 acquired mid-year in FY2021, depreciated at 20% per year (5-year useful life) alongside an original acquisition from FY2020:

Fiscal Year Annual Dep. (2nd Acq.) Factor Depreciation Posted

FY202122,460.02 × 20%0.83333,743.34
FY202222,460.02 × 20%1.00004,492.00
FY202322,460.02 × 20%1.00004,492.00
FY202422,460.02 × 20%1.00004,492.00
FY202522,460.02 × 20%0.41671,871.67
Total depreciated  19,091.01
Remaining NBV  3,369.00

The original acquisition (FY2020) is fully depreciated. The second acquisition, posted mid-FY2021, is not — because the useful life end date arrived before it had completed its own 5-year depreciation cycle.

"Can I Just Extend the Useful Life?"

This is a natural first thought, but unfortunately it does not solve the problem.

Here is why: the residual NBV comes from the fact that the second acquisition started depreciating later than the asset's ordinary depreciation start date. Extending the useful life shifts the end date forward — but the second acquisition will still be behind by the same gap. There will always be a residual, regardless of how many years you add.

Try it yourself with the 3,000 + 3,000 example above, extending to 4 years:

  • Original acquisition: fully depreciated in Y3, nothing to depreciate in Y4.
  • Second acquisition (starts Y2): depreciated in Y2, Y3, Y4 — still only 3 years out of 4 → residual of 1,000.

The gap does not close.

What Are Your Options?

Option 1: Add a New Depreciation Phase After the End of Useful Life

You can configure a new depreciation phase that activates after the useful life ends. This phase immediately depreciates the remaining NBV in full (sometimes called a "catch-up" or "residual" phase).

Pros: Clean zero NBV. No changes to current depreciation logic.
Cons: A potentially significant depreciation posting occurs after the planned useful life end, which may impact the P&L in that period.

Option 2: Switch to NBV-Based Depreciation with Remaining Useful Life

Instead of calculating depreciation from APC ÷ Useful Life, switch your depreciation key to calculate from Net Book Value ÷ Remaining Useful Life.

With this method, each period's depreciation is recalculated on the current NBV distributed over the remaining periods — so subsequent acquisitions are automatically absorbed into the ongoing depreciation, and the asset naturally converges to zero (or to scrap value) by the end of useful life.

Pros: No residual at end of useful life. Smooth, self-correcting calculation.
Cons: Depreciation amounts are not constant (they fluctuate with acquisitions and write-downs), which may not align with your accounting or reporting preferences.

Summary

Scenario Reaches Zero NBV at End of Useful Life?

Single acquisition, APC ÷ Useful LifeYes
Subsequent acquisition, APC ÷ Useful LifeNo — residual remains
Subsequent acquisition + extended useful lifeNo — residual still remains
NBV ÷ Remaining Useful Life methodYes
APC ÷ Useful Life + catch-up phaseYes (after useful life end)

The right approach depends on your company's accounting policies and reporting requirements. The system offers both paths — the decision belongs to you.

Final Thought

The behavior described in this post is standard and expected in SAP Asset Accounting. Understanding the calculation logic behind your depreciation key is essential to predict how subsequent acquisitions will behave over time.

If you are unsure which option is right for your situation, consider engaging your SAP consultant or implementation partner to evaluate the impact on your chart of depreciation before making any changes.

Have questions or a different scenario? Drop a comment below.

entry.title: Re: No option for creation of New Purchase Order Header t...
entry.content:
Hello @Punit5 

 

Thank you for your question

In Public cloud, you cannot completely create a new technical Purchase order text IDs. The available text IDs are delivered and fixed. The available configuration allows you to assign the available text IDs to the purchasing document types and maintain a customer specific text name and print sequence.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/75355ccd4602452eb6a7609e7c2b589a.html?locale=en-US

Here are areas you can consider in CBC for Header text:

  1. In Texts for Document Header, choose New Entries
  2. Ensure you maintain the following listed below:
  • Purchasing Document Type, for example NB
  • Text Object, normally EKKO or TEXT
  • Text ID, selected from the delivered value help
  • Print Sequence
  • Text name, using the business description required by the customer.
  1. Save the configuration.

The PO relevant text IDs are selected from the delivered value help. Depending on the text object and scenario, relevant IDs can use prefixes such as F or MF. You are mapping and remaining an existing ID, not creating a new technical ID.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/75355ccd4602452eb6a7609e7c2b589a.html?locale=en-US

For example, your customer may require visible labels such as:

  • Delivery Instructions
  • Quality Requirements
  • Supplier Compliance Text

You can assign available text IDs to the PO document type and maintain these descriptions in Text name.

If the requirement is to include reusable predefined context, you can create the content in the Maintain Standard Texts. Standard texts can be created and maintained with a text name, language, and supported text ID. They can then be assigned to PO outputs through the configuration and released custom logic provided for standard text determination.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/e179a83d08404e3d82447f05bb653096.html?locale=en-US

After configuration, ensure you create a new PO and check the Notes section. If the text must be printed, it is important you confirm that copied form template contains the corresponding text node and is assigned through Output parameter Determination.  

 

Let me know if you have any further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Email Notification for Credit-Blocked Sales Order Without Advanced Credit Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

My requirement is simple: when a sales order exceeds the customer’s credit limit and becomes blocked, an informational email should automatically be sent to the credit director.

The credit check works correctly, and a Documented Credit Decision is created with status 10 – New.

I also created:

  • a UKM_DCD team with the credit director as a member;
  • a Situation Type based on FIN_DCD_APPROVAL_2.

However, no email is sent because this template requires a pending DCD approval step configured through Manage Credit Management Rules, which belongs to the licensed scope 1QM – Advanced Credit Management.

Is there a standard alternative to send a simple email when a sales order is credit blocked, without activating 1QM?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Creating your first IFlow in Cloud Integration for an IBP - S/4HANA (on prem/private) integratio
entry.content:
Hi Sofiane,

Thank you for sharing this guide. We are using the latest version of the SAP IBP Add-On - Reusable Integration Flows package and have a question about the PostFetchFilterExtensionAddress.

We configured a custom Post-Fetch Extension to transform and sanitize fields before the standard mapping and upload to SAP IBP. The extension works correctly when the extraction produces a single package.

However, when MaxPackageSize causes the data to be split into multiple packages, the extension is called only for the first package. The remaining packages are posted to SAP IBP without the transformations.

Our configuration includes:

PostFetchFilterExtensionAddress="S4_IBP_MD_Product_Delta"
ParallelDataTransfer="false"

Using the Correlation ID in Monitor Message Processing, we can see multiple packages being transferred to SAP IBP, but only one Process Direct call to the Post-Fetch Extension.

Is this the expected behavior of the Generic Process Flow, or should the Post-Fetch Extension be executed for every extracted package? Has anyone experienced the same behavior or found a way to apply the extension to all packages without modifying the SAP-delivered reusable iFlow?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Prevent Outbound Delivery deletion in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Kristian2,

I tested a viable solution in Public Cloud using the BAdI Last Checks Before Saving the Delivery (LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK) together with a custom checkbox at outbound-delivery header level

SAP does not provide a separate DOCUMENTPROCESSINGMODE value for deletion. Both a normal change and complete delivery deletion are passed to the BAdI with processing mode CHANGE. However, I confirmed through Custom Logic Tracing that complete deletion can be distinguished by checking the delivery item table. During complete deletion the item table is empty while during a normal change or deletion of one item from a multi-item delivery, it still contains the remaining items

The BAdI is intended for final delivery validations immediately before saving and can prevent the operation by returning an error message

More details below:

SAP Help – Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Delivery Management

Proposed solution

I propose creating a custom checkbox at outbound-delivery header level for example:

SAP supports custom header fields for delivery documents in the Change Outbound Delivery app. The field can be displayed in the Custom Fields section of the delivery header after the relevant UI usage has been enabled and the field has been published

More details below:

SAP Help – App Extensibility for Delivery Documents

SAP Help – Process Extensibility for Delivery Documents

Once the delivery has reached or has been accepted by the 3PL warehouse the checkbox should be set to X. The BAdI can then block only complete deletion while continuing to allow normal changes and deletion of individual delivery items

Proposed BAdI logic

IF documentprocessingmode = 'CHANGE'

   AND deliverydocumentitem IS INITIAL

   AND deliverydocument-yy1_reached3pl_dlh = 'X'.

  message-messagetype = 'E'.

  message-messagetext =

    |The outbound delivery cannot be deleted because it has reached the 3PL warehouse.|.

ENDIF.

YY1_REACHED3PL_DLH is an example technical name and must be replaced with the technical name generated for your custom checkbox

SAP documents complete delivery deletion and individual item deletion as separate operations in the Change Outbound Delivery app

More details below:

SAP Help – Deleting Outbound Deliveries

SAP Help – Change Outbound Delivery

Validation performed

I tested the proposed logic with the following scenarios:

  • Checkbox selected and complete delivery deletion -> Result: The deletion was blocked and the delivery remained in the system

 

  • Checkbox selected and normal delivery change -> Result: The change was saved successfully
  • Checkbox selected and one item deleted from a multi-item delivery -> Result: The change was saved successfully
  • Checkbox not selected and complete delivery deletion -> Result: The delivery was deleted successfully

The tests confirmed that the proposed logic meets the requirement without blocking permitted changes to deliveries that have already reached the 3PL warehouse

Process recommendation

I recommend setting the checkbox automatically when confirmation is received from the 3PL warehouse, rather than relying on unrestricted manual maintenance. Otherwise, a user could clear the checkbox before deleting the delivery. Where the 3PL integration uses the Outbound Delivery (A2X) API the custom header field can be enabled for the A_OutbDeliveryHeader entity in the Custom Fields app and exposed through the API. The exact update mechanism should be aligned with the integration process used by the customer

More details below:

SAP Help – Extensibility: Outbound Delivery (A2X)

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: In RAP how to filter an association via button
entry.content:
I don't think this is possible with a RAP action alone.

The LINEITEM_REFERENCE facet is always bound to the CDS association. Even if your action returns a filtered result and you define side effects, Fiori Elements will simply refresh the association using its original CDS definition. It won't replace the association with the data returned by the action.

User clicks button
│
▼
RAP Action executes
│
▼
Returns filtered records
│
▼
Side effect refreshes UI
│
▼
Fiori reads _foreigns association again
│
▼
CDS association is executed
│
▼
Original association data is displayed

If the records should always be filtered by the current child UUID, I'd recommend modeling that directly in the CDS association (using the child UUID instead of the root UUID if that fits your data model).

If the filtering should only happen after pressing the button, I'd either:

  • navigate to a separate projection/list that accepts the UUID as a parameter, or
  • implement the filtering in a UI controller extension.

Just out of curiosity, is there a reason why the association is based on the root UUID instead of the child UUID? If each child has its own UUID, changing the association might simplify the whole requirement.

entry.title: SDI sunset and migration to RTI
entry.content:
SDI sunset and migration to RTI

If you are running SAP IBP Order-Based Planning (OBP) today using SAP Smart Data Integration (SDI), this blog is for you. As of December 31, 2027, SAP HANA Smart Data Integration (SDI) will no longer be supported in SAP IBP Order-Based Planning. Customers on SAP7 + SDI must migrate to RTI before this date.

The good news - SAP has built a clear migration path, dedicated tooling, and a strategic target architecture to move you forward. This blog covers what is changing, what your options are, and what tools are available to help you migrate to Real-Time Integration (RTI). 

Sunset timelines

SAP has signaled this direction progressively over several releases, giving customers much needed lead time to plan migration:

  • IBP 2111 - SAP7F (the RTI-based flexible planning area) made available. New OBP customers directed to start on SAP7F + RTI directly.
  • IBP 2211 - SAP7F became the default for all new OBP projects. SAP7 retained only for existing live customers.
  • IBP 2602 - "Migrate Configuration to SAP7F" app delivered — SAP's dedicated tool for existing SDI customers to migrate their planning area configuration.
  • IBP 2605 - App renamed "Migrate Configuration" and enhanced to support direct migration to the Harmonized Planning Area (I_SAPIBP2).
  • December 31, 2027 — SDI support ends. All customers must have completed migration to RTI by this date.

Integration roadmap for SAP IBP

The SDI sunset is part of a broader architectural direction for IBP. Below diagram shows various integration options available for SAP IBP.

Looking ahead, primary tool for integrating Master data, Stocks and Orders is SAP RTI, which will be complimented by SAP CI for integrating time series data. For more details in future roadmap, please refer IBP Integration strategy presented in March 2025. Following SAP help portal page helps you find right integration tool for your business needs: Finding the Right Integration Tool | SAP Help Portal.

RTI advantages over SDI

RTI is a fundamentally better integration architecture for OBP, and staying on SDI is not just a compliance risk by end of 2027, it is an innovation risk starting today. Below table compares SDI and RTI capabilities.

Dimension

SDI

RTI

Data transfer

Batch-based, scheduled jobs

Near real-time, event-driven

Staging tables

/IBP/EXT* tables required

No interim tables

Planning area

SAP7 (static/external master data)

SAP7F / I_SAPIBP2 (flexible master data)

Extensibility

Static model, limited custom attributes

Flexible — custom attributes, CIF BADIs

New features

No new features being delivered

All future OBP innovations

Data reconciliation

Manual

Dedicated Fiori app (since IBP 2202)

As RTI is available with SAP7F and I_SAPIBP2 planning area, migration opens up a growing set of OBP capabilities that are simply not available on SDI, and this list expands with every IBP release. If any of the following are on your roadmap, RTI is a prerequisite:

Capability

SDI / SAP7

RTI / SAP7F or I_SAPIBP2

Shelf-Life Planning

Not available

Available (SAP7F required)

Subcontracting in OBP

Not available

Available

Batch management integration

Not available

On roadmap for RTI

Future PPIMs (beyond 1, 3, 9)

Not available

All future PPIMs

Sales Scheduling Agreements

Not available

RTI required when released

Quality Inspection Lots

Not available

RTI required when released

Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP)

Not available

Available

Harmonized Planning Area (HPA)

Not available

I_SAPIBP2 required

Supported ERP Releases for RTI

RTI is supported from SAP ECC EHP4 onwards and from SAP S/4HANA 2020 onwards. For the complete list of supported releases and required notes, refer to SAP Note 3106619.

 Understanding planning areas and associated migration paths

As mentioned above, SDI is sunsetting by end of 2027, and customers currently using SDI need to migrate to RTI. For Order based planning, we have 2 planning area models that are compatible with SAP RTI. Understanding these area models is essential for choosing the right migration path.

I_SAPIBP2 (HPA): SAP's newest and most comprehensive planning area. Combines time-series (SAPIBP1) and OBP (SAP7F) capabilities into a single harmonized model. All SAP's future investment is primarily focused on harmonized planning area. Best for customers also using time-series planning.

 

SAP7F: Order based planning replacement for SAP, introduced in 2111 release. Based on flexible master data with configurable, extensible MDTs. Supports all current OBP scenarios: shelf life, subcontracting, CBP, and synchronized planning. Though, this is still a valid and available target for OBP-focused customers, SAP’s recommendation is to migrate to I_SAPIBP2 directly, to avoid double migration efforts, as all future investments from SAP will focus on I_SAPIBP2.

 

SAP Recommendation: Future functional enhancements will focus on the Harmonized Planning Area (I_SAPIBP2) first. SAP recommends evaluating whether direct migration to I_SAPIBP2/HPA is applicable before defaulting to SAP7F.

 

Migration path available for customers

 SAP provides two migration paths for customers still using SDI. These are listed in detail in SAP Note: 3311715

 

Path 1: SAP7 / SAP7F → I_SAPIBP2 (HPA) : Move to SAP's newest unified planning area, combining OBP and time-series in one model. Best for customers using time-series IBP planning, or who want the longest runway for new feature access. SAP recommends evaluating this path first.

 

Path 2: SAP7 → SAP7F (RTI) : Focused OBP migration. Moves from legacy static model to flexible master data while keeping your broader IBP architecture unchanged. Best for customers wanting a contained, well-scoped migration with minimal disruption. SAP7F is a fully supported long-term target.

Below table calls out recommended path for different scenarios:

Your Situation

Recommended Path

New OBP implementation

Start directly on I_SAPIBP2 / HPA

Existing SDI, OBP only, want minimal scope

SAP7F + RTI

Existing SDI, also use time-series IBP planning

Consider I_SAPIBP2 directly

With the target defined, the next question is: what does migration entail? The answer depends partly on how customized your current SAP7 implementation is — but SAP has built dedicated tooling to support every step of the process.

The Migrate Configuration App

The cornerstone of the migration is the Migrate Configuration app (SAP Note 3311715). This SAP Fiori app guides the migration of your planning area configuration from SAP7 to SAP7F, or from SAP7/SAP7F to I_SAPIBP2. The app is available to all customers, but access must be requested by raising a case on component SCM-IBP-CNF-M2F. Once access is granted, the migration guide and necessary business catalogs are provided.

What the App Migrates Automatically

For SAP7 → SAP7F migrations: Planning area sub objects (attributes, planning levels, key figures, etc.), planning filters, permission filters, planning view templates and favorites (Excel add-in), analytics charts, dashboards, custom alerts, Intelligent Visibility profiles, copy operator profiles, key figure groups, Planner Workspaces (workbooks and variants), application job templates, and navigations to other systems.

Additionally, for SAP7/SAP7F → I_SAPIBP2 migrations: All the above, plus forecast models, forecast automation profiles, ABC/XYZ segmentation profiles, transportation load views and profiles, master data views, realignment projects, correlation analysis profiles, automated reconciliation profiles, planning notes, rules for master data maintenance, and settings for product lifecycle.

What requires manual work for migration

While the app automates a great deal, some elements require manual attention — and it is important to plan for these upfront:

  • BADIs: Existing SDI/IBP Add-On BADI implementations cannot be directly reused. They must be reimplemented using the RTI CIF BADIs (search for CIF*IBP in SE18 in S/4, or install the relevant notes for ECC). The design logic from existing BADIs helps reduce reimplementation effort, but this is not a copy-paste exercise.
  • RTI connectivity: SAP Cloud Connector and SAP BTP Connectivity service must be configured to establish the connection between IBP and your ERP/S4 system.
  • CIF Integration Models: New CIF integration models must be defined in ECC/S4 to activate RTI data transfer.
  • Job scheduling: The app migrates application job templates but does not schedule them — rescheduling must be done manually after migration.
  • Structural planning area adjustments: Some manual changes in the Planning Areas app are always required after migration. For example, the migration tool does not automatically change the root attribute of planning levels.

Migration effort

Migration effort depends on the customization of the existing SAP7 based planning area. If your SAP7 planning area is close to standard SAP7 content and kept current with regular compare/update across IBP releases, migration effort is   minimal (few days). However, if your existing SAP7 based planning area contains high number of custom key figures, extensive BADI implementations, planning area not regularly synchronized with latest SAP7 sample, then migration may need higher efforts up to couple of months.

Note: Use the Simulate Migration function before committing. The Migrate Configuration app's simulation generates detailed application logs showing all warnings, errors, and items requiring manual attention — without making any changes to your system. This gives you a realistic picture of scope and effort before you start.

For detailed pre-requisites and steps please refer SAP Help documentation: SAP7 / SAP7F → I_SAPIBP2 (HPA)  and SAP7 → SAP7F (RTI) 

Available Tools and Resources

SAP has provided a comprehensive set of tools and resources to support customers through every stage of this migration. Here is what is available and where to find it.

1) Migrate Configuration App

The primary migration tool. Guides the end-to-end migration of your planning area configuration with simulation, step-by-step execution, detailed application logs, and rollback capability. Available to all customers — request access via a case on SCM-IBP-CNF-M2F.

2) Key SAP Notes

 

SAP Note

Purpose

SAP Note 3311715

Central migration note — start here. Covers SAP7 → SAP7F and SAP7/SAP7F → I_SAPIBP2

SAP Note 3110007

RTI Information & Restrictions, including the RTI Setup Checklist

SAP Note 3106619

RTI Installation Notes — full matrix of supported ECC and S/4HANA releases

3) SAP Help Portal Documentation

  • Migrating Configuration to a Planning Area Based on Flexible Master Data — complete SAP7 → SAP7F migration guide
  • Migrating Configuration to a Planning Area Based on I_SAPIBP2 — complete SAP7/SAP7F → I_SAPIBP2 migration guide
  • Real-Time Integration with SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA — RTI setup and configuration reference
  • FAQs: SAP IBP Real-Time Integration and Transition from SDI — comprehensive Q&A covering all aspects of the transition

4) Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS) Workflow

SAP provides a guided, semi-automated CIAS workflow specifically for RTI connectivity setup: com.sap.ibp.rti. This workflow uses tenant-specific information such as target URLs and reusable configuration settings to streamline the technical setup between IBP and your ERP/S4 system, significantly reducing manual configuration effort and the risk of setup errors. SAP strongly recommends using this workflow for RTI connectivity setup. 

What about Time-Series planning integration?

A question that often arises is whether RTI affects time-series planning — demand planning, S&OP, inventory optimization. The short answer is: it does not.

RTI applies exclusively to Order-Based Planning. All time-series planning integration continues to use SAP CI and SAP CIDS for data integration. If you are running both OBP and time-series planning in IBP today, your time-series integration setup is unaffected by the SDI → RTI migration.

The one scenario where this change is if you migrate to I_SAPIBP2 (HPA) in that case, both OBP (via RTI) and time-series planning are brought together within a single unified planning area, which is one of the key architectural benefits of the HPA migration path.

 S/4HANA Upgrade dependency on SDI Migration:

The SDI sunset deadline is independent of your S/4HANA version. SDI integration between IBP and S/4HANA operates via the IBP Add-On installed on the ERP side. When you upgrade S/4HANA, the Add-On continues to handle SDI connectivity — no IBP migration is required as part of the S/4 upgrade project itself.  Upgrading to a newer version of S/4HANA does not force migration to RTI.

Activity

Description

Dependency

S/4HANA upgrade (e.g. 1909 → 2025)

Upgrading your ERP system version

Independent of IBP SDI migration

SDI → RTI migration

Moving IBP OBP from SAP7+SDI to SAP7F/I_SAPIBP2+RTI

Required by Dec 31, 2027 — independent of S/4 version

 

BTP Licensing

A question that frequently comes up is whether RTI requires new licensing. 

Scenario

What is Needed

Already have an SAP BTP subscription

No additional subscription needed for RTI

No BTP subscription yet

A BTP subscription is required for the SAP Connectivity service and Cloud Connector. Contact your SAP account executive or visit store.sap.com

No extra license on the ERP/S4HANA side-

The RTI components are integrated into native S/4 CIF objects — no extra add-on installation or additional license is required on the ERP/S4HANA side.

Conclusion

December 2027 sunset for SDI is approaching fast; it is recommended to migrate to RTI well before the sunset date. SAP has provided clear paths and tools for migration. Please plan your migration based on your business requirements and roadmap. You can also contact engage-ibp@sap.com for direct engagement with the IBP Customer Engagement team.

entry.title: Re: Apply Machine Learning on Incident data set in excel...
entry.content:
It really depends on what you're trying to achieve with the incident data. Different use cases require different ML models.

For example:

  • If you want to predict the number of incidents over the next few weeks or months, a time series forecasting model (such as Prophet or ARIMA) would be a good fit.
  • If you want to predict the priority or category of a new incident, then it's a classification problem (Random Forest, XGBoost, etc.).
  • If you want to estimate the resolution time, that's a regression problem.
  • If you're trying to identify similar incidents without predefined labels, clustering algorithms like K-Means can help.

Since your data is in Excel, I'd first spend some time understanding the dataset and cleaning it before choosing a model. The quality of the input data usually has a much bigger impact than the choice of algorithm.

If you're on SAP BTP, you can also have a look at SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad if you plan to build and deploy ML models.

Could you share what columns are available in the dataset and what kind of prediction or insight you're expected to deliver? That would make it easier to recommend the most suitable approach.

Hi

First, I would recommend to reach out to your account team and start there. There are conversion onboarding materials and they can help get you started and discuss contracts, etc.

Please check this note: 3643616 - Summarization of features that require the 3-system landscape of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

and: The following presentation summarizes the main features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which are highlighted as relevant after conversion from a 2-system landscape to a 3-system landscape: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - 3SL/CBC Conversion Program - Delta Features

For example, there is a FAQ here (hopefully the link works for you)

Also, there are several similar questions in the community: 

Solved: Has anyone recently converted from 2SL to 3SL syst... - SAP Community

There are quite a few benefits moving to a 3SL system, for example, Developer Extensibility and the Parallel Line. Neither are available with 2SL.

For your specific questions:

1. I don't have the answer for this one.

2. and 3. Please check the Developer Extensibility options for Exposing APIs

Also check: Building and Publishing External APIs in SAP S/4HA... - SAP Community

and Developing API's in S/4 HANA Cloud

4. I took a quick look at your list. For the ones that are released Public APIs, I would expect them to work with no changes in 3SL (unless there is a case where a given API was deprecated, or changed, but I believe that would also affect 2SL). Many of your custom interfaces appear to be exposed custom CDS Views. We would expect those to continue to work as well after migration, but please check the FAQ as there might be some rewiring to be aware of. One note that is important is you would need to update the systems that use these endpoints as the URL and system name would change when you move to 3SL.

5. Upgrade timelines and restrictions are a little different, the transport and activation processes are a bit different. But you should find these are improvements

I hope this helps

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Problem with discarding entity using CAP as a proxy o...
entry.content:
From your description, I don't think the Discard action itself is the problem. It sounds like the subsequent READ isn't behaving the way Fiori Elements expects.

After a successful Discard, FE typically issues one or more GET requests to rebind the page to the active instance. If your CAP service simply forwards req.query to the RAP service, it may still be forwarding a query that targets IsActiveEntity = false (the draft), which no longer exists after the discard.

I'd start by checking the actual OData request that reaches the RAP service after Discard. In particular, verify whether the request is still using IsActiveEntity=false or whether the key predicate changes after the discard.

The other thing I'd verify is whether your CAP proxy is forwarding all draft-related query options and key predicates unchanged. FE relies on the standard draft protocol, so even a small difference in how the proxy handles the request can result in the rebind failing.

Since you're implementing your own proxy instead of using CAP's native draft support (@odata.draft.enabled), you may need to explicitly handle the post-discard read scenario so that it resolves to the active entity.

Out of curiosity, if you call the RAP V4 service directly (without the CAP proxy), does Discard followed by the automatic READ work as expected? That would quickly tell you whether the issue is in the RAP service or in the proxy layer.

entry.title: DI API Disconnecting with Error: You are not connected to a company
entry.content:
I Have a DI API Windwos Service that works fine for 3-5 days with no errors, and then i get the You are not connected to a company, error.

I haven't  found i solution for this.

I am Using .NET 10 with various various threads using a singleton DI API  Company object Instance, i don't know if calling the GetBusinessObject method is not thread safe, the same with Recordset.DoQuery and adding or update any objects or services.

here is a snippet of the singleton

    internal sealed class DIAPI : IDIAPI
    {
        public Company Company { get; private set; }

        private readonly string _path;
        private readonly ILogger<DIAPI> _logger;

        public DIAPI(ILogger<DIAPI> logger, string path)
        {
            _logger = logger;
            _path = path;
            Company = new Company();
            (bool conected, string error) = Connect(_path);
            if (!conected)
            {
                _logger.LogError("erro ao tentar conectar: {erro}", error);
            }
        }

        private (bool connected , string error) Connect(string path)
        {
            if (Company is not null && Company.Connected)
                return (true, "");

            // parametros de conexão
            Conf conf = Conf.GetConfiguration(path);
            if(conf.DBType is null)
                throw new Exception("Tipo de banco de dados não informado na configuração.");

            string erro = string.Empty;
            Company = new()
            {
                LicenseServer = conf.LicenceServer,
                CompanyDB = conf.CompanyDB,
                UserName = conf.UserName,
                Password = conf.Password,
                DbServerType = (BoDataServerTypes)conf.DBType, // SQL 2019 é 15
                SLDServer = conf.SLDServer,
                Server = conf.Server,
                DbUserName = conf.UserDB,
                DbPassword = conf.PassDB,
                language = BoSuppLangs.ln_Portuguese_Br,
                UseTrusted = false
            };

            if (Company.Connect() != 0)
            {
                erro = $"{Company.GetLastErrorCode()} | {Company.GetLastErrorDescription()}";
                return (false, erro);
            }

            return (true, "");
        }
}

here is an exeple of injecting the singleton in a class

public Email_Queue (ILogger<Email_Queue> logger, IDIAPI api) { _api = api; _logger = logger; }


and using like this for exemple inside the many threads.

 

Recordset recordset = _api.Company.GetBusinessObject(BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);


 Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition - Job Scheduler failing while running the job for Milestone Award
entry.content:
I'm looking at it with product team and in jiras. 
Can you share the details of your milestone award and also can you share the full logs from provisioning?

 

 

Additionally please review the below from Claude: 

 

I couldn't find a public SAP KBA matching that exact stack trace (sqlToGetUserId is null in SQLWithBindVars.getSql()), so this looks like it hasn't been documented publicly yet — but the error itself is diagnosable from how the "Identifies eligible users for milestone awards" job works internally.

What's happening

That job builds a dynamic SQL query to pull the list of eligible userIds based on how you've configured Eligibility on the Milestone Program (either "Eligibility Rules" using an EC rule on the spotAwardEligibility base object, or "Dynamic Groups"). The SQLWithBindVars object is supposed to be populated with that generated SQL and then executed — the NPE means the job never got that far: it tried to call .getSql() on an object that was never constructed. That points to the eligibility configuration itself failing to resolve into a valid query, not a data/runtime issue with a specific user.

Where to look, in order of likelihood

  1. Dynamic Groups path (most common cause of this specific NPE):
    • Open the Milestone Program → Eligibility settings and confirm which mode is selected.
    • If Dynamic Groups: go to Admin Center → Manage Data → find the Dynamic Group object tied to this program and check it actually has criteria rows defined. An empty/unpublished Dynamic Group, or one whose base object criteria reference a field that's since been removed/renamed in the corporate data model, will fail to generate SQL — that's exactly the null-SQL symptom.
    • Check the group hasn't been deleted or is in a draft/unpublished state while still being referenced by the program.
  2. Eligibility Rules path:
    • If using EC rules on spotAwardEligibility, open Configure Business Rules and validate the rule still compiles cleanly — a rule referencing a deleted/renamed MDF field or foundation object will silently fail to generate a base query rather than throwing a rule-validation error at save time.
  3. Re-save to force regeneration: Sometimes the cached rule/group reference is stale even if the config looks fine in the UI. Go into the program's Eligibility step, re-select the same Dynamic Group or Rule, and Save again — this forces the program to re-persist the association and can clear a broken cached reference.
  4. Check for orphaned/legacy config: If this program was cloned or migrated from another one, confirm the eligibility object wasn't left pointing at a group/rule ID that belongs to a different program type (Spot Award vs Milestone use different base object types even though they share the spotAwardEligibility rule type).

If none of that resolves it

Since this is an InternalServerErrorException (category InternalError) — not a validation error — with a raw NPE in server-side RBP DAO code, there's a real chance the eligibility config is in a state the UI allowed but the backend can't handle (a known class of bug in Milestone Awards, which is still a relatively new module). If steps 1–3 don't clear it, this is worth an incident to SAP Product Support with the exact stack trace and component LOD-SF-RNR — they can check server logs for which program/group ID it's failing against, which you can't see from the client side.

@manish_rustagi462677

entry.title: Re: Job Approval in SmartRecruiters: Overview, Features, and Configuration Guide
entry.content:
Hi @SimonKjge ,

You cannot directly create custom questions or attachment fields in SmartRecruiters. However, you can create custom fields on the requisition form to capture the required information. For attachments, you can use the standard Attachments section available on the requisition form. As of now, SmartRecruiters does not support creating custom attachment fields. Therefore, any documents that need to be uploaded by the hiring leader must be captured using the standard attachment field provided by the system.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Qeries related to Power BI integration with S/4hana
entry.content:
Hi there,

Yes, Power BI can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Direct access through the native SAP HANA connector is one option, but it requires database connectivity, the HANA client, network configuration, and appropriate database permissions.

You may also evaluate Power BI Connector for SAP, available on SAP Store:

https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/partners/metrica-software-inc-power-bi-connector-for-sap.html

It connects through SAP BTP and activated S/4HANA OData services without requiring direct HANA database access. Users can select services, entities, fields, parameters, and server-side filters, then connect Power BI to the generated OData endpoint.

This workflow supports scheduled and incremental refresh. It depends on the OData services available in your SAP environment and is not equivalent to HANA SQL or native HANA DirectQuery.

The connector requires a separate subscription. SAP and BTP licensing requirements should be confirmed with your SAP account manager.

A 30-day free trial is available.

Documentation:
https://metricasoftware.com/docs/sap/

Cheers,

Anton

entry.title: Re: Auto Salary Calculation Not Available in SmartRecruit...
entry.content: As you are aware, this cannot be achieved directly in SmartRecruiters. However, it can be implemented through a custom integration. One possible approach is to use middleware such as SAP Cloud Integration (CPI). You can create a custom MDF object in SuccessFactors to store all the required compensation fields, along with the criteria used for the calculations, such as grade, location, and any other relevant attributes required by SmartRecruiters. The business rules for the compensation calculations can be configured within the custom MDF object. Once the rules are in place, the required data (such as Offered CTC, Grade, Location, etc.) can be sent from SmartRecruiters to the custom MDF object through the integration. Based on the configured business rules, the remaining compensation components will be calculated automatically. After the calculations are completed, the calculated compensation details can be sent back from the custom MDF object to SmartRecruiters, where they can be displayed or used in the recruitment process.

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters to SAP SuccessFactors EC Integration...
entry.content:
To map fields between SmartRecruiters and SuccessFactors, you can use the Integration Hub available in SmartRecruiters. However, not all fields are currently supported for mapping. For example, pay components and certain country-specific fields are not supported through the standard Integration Hub. For fields that are not supported, you will need to build a custom integration using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) or any other middleware used in your landscape. This custom integration can be used to transfer the unsupported fields between SmartRecruiters and SuccessFactors.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters - Assign Hiring Team Roles in Bulk /...
entry.content:
Hi @Arisp1 ,

If you are using the Hiring Team, users must be added individually, as SmartRecruiters does not currently provide an option to add multiple users at once. Alternatively, you can create an Access Group based on your business requirements. Users who are members of the Access Group will automatically receive access to all requisitions and applications that match the criteria defined for that group. Although the Access Group itself is not visible on the requisition form, it can effectively meet your client's requirement by automatically granting access to requisitions and applications based on the configured criteria, eliminating the need to add users manually to each requisition.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

It's a great opportunity to learn from experts, ask questions, and gain practical skills that can help keep your implementation on track.

entry.title: Re: Calling offline Cap Service, throwing error on passin...
entry.content:
I don't believe Offline supports storing Action Import calls.  I believe Function Imports can be stored offline but I have not looked into either lately.  

If you are using offline I would suggest just using the standard OData CRUD operations and adjust your service if necessary.

entry.title: Re: error on calling a odata service from sap mdk app
entry.content:
I think your action override is incorrect.  I believe the individual parameters for the Call Function are under a Parameters property in the structure of the action.  So in your executeAction call with the overrides you need to wrap all your properties with the Parameters block.

await context.executeAction(
   {
      "Name": "/SyncApp/Actions/Docket/CreateDeliveryNote_G/CreateDeliveryNote_Online.action",
      "Properties": 
      {
         "Parameters": {
            // "Parameters": payload
            "contractNo": payload.contractNo,
            "custPoNumber": payload.custPoNumber,
            "deliveryDate": payload.deliveryDate,
            "deliveryTime": payload.deliveryTime,
            "locationText": payload.locationText,
            "notes": payload.notes,
            "plantId": payload.plantId,
            "plantName": payload.plantName,
            "products": payload.products,
            "services": payload.services,
            "shipToId": payload.shipToId,
            "shipToName1": payload.shipToName1,
            "shipToName2": payload.shipToName2,
            "soldToId": payload.soldToId,
            "soldToName1": payload.soldToName1,
            "soldToName2": payload.soldToName2,
            "shotId": payload.shotId,
            "vehicleOperators": payload.vehicleOperators
         }
      }
   }
);

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 23rd, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/abap-development-tools-for-visual-studio-code-version-1-1-released/ba-p/14421143

  • Developer News on CAP AI Plugin: https://community.sap.com/t5/developer-news/sap-developer-news-july-9th-2026/ba-p/14436806
  • Developer News on Change Tracking 2.0 Plugin: https://community.sap.com/t5/developer-news/sap-developer-news-for-april-30th-2026/ba-p/14387145

SAP Business Client 8.10 Joule for SAPGUI
