  • SAPGUI Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-gui-for-windows-8-10-is-coming-general-availability-as-of-16th-of-july/ba-p/14434135

SAP Business AI: Release 2026-Q2

  • Release Highlights Q2 2026: https://news.sap.com/2026/07/sap-business-ai-release-highlights-q2-2026/

SAP Connect Day for UX in Newtown Square September 2nd

  • Event Page: https://events.sap.com/noam-connect-day-for-ux-newtown-square-pa/en_us/home.html

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1

1:06 What’s New in SAP Build: Q1 & Q2 2026 Release Highlights

5:53 SAP Business Client 8.10 Joule for SAPGUI

7:10 SAP Business AI: Release Highlights Q2

10:01 Connect Day for UX in Newtown Square Sept 2

Transcription

00;00;01;19 - 00;00;09;10
Announcer
This is the SAP Developer News for July the 23rd, 2026.

00;00;09;12 - 00;00;09;23
Mamikee Kanneh
ABAP

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;43;28
Mamikee Kanneh
Developing Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1 is now available. Bring in more development capabilities and usability improvement to the extension. This release adds support for several highly requested ABAP object types, including classic ABAP source code program, and include function groups and function modules, as well as additional dictionary and wrap objects. Version 1.1 also expands the available MCP tools, giving AI coding agents more ways to interact with ABAP systems and support development tasks directly within Visual Studio Code.

00;00;43;28 - 00;01;07;03
Mamikee Kanneh
And based on the feedback from the first release, we introduced an additional set of usability improvements to make everyday development workflow smoother. This is another major step towards expanding the ABAP development experience in Visual Studio Code. Now to explore the full list of new features and updates included in version 1.1. Head to the block post links in the description below.

00;01;07;05 - 00;01;44;03
Shrini Neelamegam
Hello everyone! Q1 and Q2 of 2026 brought massive updates across SAP Build covering Work Zone, CAP, SAP Process Automation, and the Joule Studio for development. Let's dive in. In SAP Build Work Zone. Customizing your experience is easier with Myspace. A single page to organize all your favorite control on the AI front. Next generation is now integrated into the Text widget toolbar, so you can draft or refine Copy on the fly.

00;01;44;06 - 00;02;19;11
Shrini Neelamegam
Plus, Joule Work Zone Advance can now create entire workspaces from scratch and quickly locate specific content on the mobile front. SAP Mobile Start is now rebranded as Mobile App. It features a refreshed look. Voice dictation for prompts and seamless single sign on for web apps in version 3.2. Version 3.1 brings dynamic spaces to group your key tools, pinning options, and custom admin support channels.

00;02;19;14 - 00;02;53;12
Shrini Neelamegam
Finally, MDK 26.6 introduces pre onboarding, compliance checks, stronger security, and embedded AI capabilities. Over in Cap, we have two major plugin updates. Antonio previously gave a deep dive into the cap AI plugin, and DJ Adams covered the Change Tracking 2.2 plugin in an earlier developer news episode. Check out their segments link below for the full backstory and the latest details for SAP UI5

00;02;53;14 - 00;03;34;12
Shrini Neelamegam
Keeping apps modern is much easier. The new UI modernization plugin automates legacy upgrades like replacing deprecated APIs, and works with Claude Code and Copilot as well. The updated unify MCP server adds three new tools to help LLMs build UI integration cards, following best practices for accessibility. Business users can already adapt theory apps at runtime using key user adaptation, but when business requirements get too complex for low code tools, you can now seamlessly bridge the gap to developer extensibility.

00;03;34;15 - 00;04;08;08
Shrini Neelamegam
When creating an adaptation project in SAP Business Application Studio, simply select the brand new Import Key User Changes option to pull those runtime edits straight into your DevOps space. And if you are building SAP unify apps with TypeScript. Be sure to check out our new developer guide detailing the major transitions happening across the TypeScript ecosystem. Next up, let's talk about SAP Build Process Automation.

00;04;08;11 - 00;04;40;21
Shrini Neelamegam
Monitoring your workflows gets a major upgrade with instance level attributes. You can now attach real business context directly to running processes, giving operational teams much clearer visibility into key details and helping them make faster, better informed decisions. To help you stay ahead of operational issues, you can now set up proactive email notifications for processing failures or skip instances.

00;04;40;23 - 00;05;15;07
Shrini Neelamegam
Plus, new environment filters make governance simpler by letting you view or restrict process visibility based on where your workflow slip, like dev, test or production. Handling errors is now much smoother thanks to new configurable retry mechanisms and structured exception handling right at the action level, so temporary hiccups don't derail your entire process. Finally, if you are looking to dive into Joule Studio, we have got you great news!

00;05;15;08 - 00;05;40;13
Shrini Neelamegam
The brand new free learning course Introducing Joule Studio is now live on SAP learning. This course gives you the complete big picture showing how Jules studio fits into the SAP business AI platform. What you can achieve with your and you'll work, and how to build agents and orchestrate end to end workflows with native.

00;05;40;15 - 00;05;53;21
Shrini Neelamegam
You can find the link to the full Release Highlights blog post, along with direct link to every feature and the learning journey we cover today down in the video description below. Thanks for watching.

00;05;53;23 - 00;06;20;15
Thomas Jung
Last week when many of the developer advocates were together for Code Connect. We were at dinner one evening and we were talking about possible demos for SAP TechEd and we kind of joked about building a Joule interface for the SAP GUI. And lo and behold, this week I woke up and read an article about the new version of the SAP Business Client.

00;06;20;16 - 00;06;45;05
Thomas Jung
The wraps around the SAP GUI, and sure enough, that's one of the major features that the brand new 8.1 version of the business brings to the SAP experience. The business client, formerly the NetWeaver Business Client, has long been a tool for wrapping around the SAP GUI and providing additional navigation and functionality. Used to be that you used it for the sidecar widgets.

00;06;45;08 - 00;07;11;17
Thomas Jung
Now it can be used to wrap the Joule interface and allow Joule interaction with SAP GUI transactions. So if that sounds like something you'd be interested in. Check out the links below in the show notes to download and set up the newest version of both SAP GUI and the SAP Business Client to try out that new feature.

00;07;11;19 - 00;07;45;13
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
In SAP Business AI platform 2026 Q2 focuses on three technical areas building AI solutions, improving model reasoning and observability, and governing agents at scale in the build layer. The new Joule 2D unifies design, extension and production of AI agents, apps and workflows. It generates requirements and technical specs from context. Applies a well based guardrails to AI coding assistance under SAP managed controls, and support third party or SAP models.

00;07;45;15 - 00;08;19;29
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
Also in build, the SAP S/4 HANA custom code migration agent now generally available, automates above migration from IC to S for it runs above test cockpit checks across custom code, interprets findings, categorizes issues and applies deterministic fixes plus AI based changes with confidence scores in contextualize intrusion. The generative AI hub adds a batch API for high volume foundational model workloads, with asynchronous job submission and results written to an object store.

00;08;20;02 - 00;08;52;05
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
The inference observability service centralizes logging of prompts, responses and context, supports response rating, and enables data set export for fine tuning and benchmarking. Speech to speech recognition is now available for end to end voice experiences as well as SAP document AI hot model selection for document processing. Three new standard document types and configurable document level confidence ranges shown in the document header.

00;08;52;08 - 00;09;27;04
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
SAP domain models trained on SAP domain knowledge help create clean, core compliant extensions in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Ariba from natural language and query customer data grounded in data models and business contexts. This models run under the hood and are not directly exposed to customers and partners in the governance. The SAP AI Agent Hub provides a single control plane for AI agents, large language models, and MCP servers.

00;09;27;09 - 00;10;01;23
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
It automates asset discovery across Microsoft, Google, AWS, ServiceNow, and SAP. AI core delivers structured governments governance assessment and uses a verification badge integrated with runtime solutions to control approved agents and MCP servers. Future plans cover runtime observability, identity and access control agent in protest mining and workforce impact mapping. Full release highlights for business IQ to 2026 are available via the link in the description.

00;10;01;26 - 00;10;29;00
Thomas Jung
I want to tell you about the upcoming Connect Day for User Experience. This is planned for September 2nd in the SAP Newtown Square offices. So if you're on the east coast of the US, this is an opportunity for you to come together with your fellow SAP developers and UX designers, as well as experts from SAP to discuss all things user experience.

00;10;29;02 - 00;10;50;11
Thomas Jung
There's so much happening in this area right now with the impact of AI on both the creation of user experience, as well as embedding AI into the user experience. It's going to be so much to talk about that day, and we wanted to tell you well in advance so you can mark your calendar. We'll remind you again on the developer news is we get closer to that date.

00;10;50;13 - 00;11;04;28
Thomas Jung
I plan to be there for that event. I'm coming into town, and I think Rich Holman from the Developer Advocates team will be there as well. So if you're interested in talking to us and others about user experience, this is going to be a great opportunity. We look forward to seeing you there.

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Marssel Vilaça
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, criada para compartilhar as histórias das pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade. Fui convidado por @SimoneAzevedo para participar desta série e compartilhar um pouco da minha trajetória na SAP Community Curitiba.

Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

Sou Marssel Vilaça e acredito que comunidades existem para aproximar pessoas. Entrei na SAP Community em 2007 procurando respostas para dúvidas técnicas durante meus primeiros anos como consultor SAP. O que eu não imaginava era que aquele espaço mudaria impactaria tanto na minha carreira. Tenho um blog pessoal publicado aqui nesta série BIF.

Com o passar dos anos comecei a responder perguntas, escrever blogs, ministrar palestras, produzir vídeos, organizar eventos e incentivar outras pessoas a também compartilharem conhecimento.

Hoje atuo como SAP Champion, curador da SAP Community Recife, São Paulo e também  da SAP Community Curitiba desde suas primeiras iniciativas.

O que mais me motiva...

Compartilhar conhecimento multiplica oportunidades. Talvez o maior patrimônio da SAP Community nunca tenha sido a tecnologia. Sempre foram as pessoas.

Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Grande parte das oportunidades profissionais que tive nasceu de pessoas que conheceram meu trabalho através da comunidade. Muitos clientes, projetos, palestras, treinamentos e amizades surgiram depois que comecei a compartilhar conhecimento de forma voluntária.

Momento especial

Sem dúvidas, foi muito especial poder contribuir para os primeiros passos da SAP Community Curitiba. Ver essa comunidade crescer, reunir pessoas tão engajadas e realizar eventos de alto nível mostra que, quando o trabalho é voluntário e feito com propósito, os resultados aparecem naturalmente.

Conecte-se comigo:

https://about.me/mabv

Convidados para continuar a série
Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido: @LuizSouza , @AdrianoCWB

Marssel Vilaça

entry.title: Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management - What's New in 2608
entry.content:
Hello SAP Supply Chain Management community!

As global supply chains continue to evolve in complexity and scale, organizations need solutions that can adapt quickly while maintaining operational excellence. The 2608 release cycle brings a comprehensive set of enhancements across the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio, designed to address the real-world challenges you face every day, from improving planning accuracy and manufacturing efficiency to enhancing collaboration across your extended supply chain network.

This release demonstrates SAP's continued commitment to innovation across the entire supply chain portfolio. Whether you're looking to optimize your planning processes with SAP Integrated Business Planning, streamline manufacturing operations with SAP Digital Manufacturing, or enhance collaboration through SAP Business Network solutions, the 2608 release delivers meaningful capabilities that drive tangible business value.

In this post, we highlight what’s new across key products and, more importantly, point you to the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management, where you can explore detailed feature descriptions, walk-thru simulations, and enablement content to support your adoption journey. Let's explore what's new across each solution area.

 

  • What's New in Supply Chain Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Business Network for Logistics
  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration
  • SAP Logistics Management
  • Key Takeaways
  • Looking Ahead
  • Connect with Us

 

What's New in Supply Chain Management

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The 2608 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces several enhancements focused on improving supply chain visibility and operational efficiency. New capabilities in inventory management enable better tracking and control of stock levels across multiple locations, while improvements to procurement processes streamline supplier collaboration and purchase order management. The release also includes enhanced analytics capabilities that provide deeper insights into supply chain performance metrics, enabling more informed decision-making. Integration improvements ensure seamless data flow between S/4HANA and other SAP supply chain solutions, creating a more unified technology landscape. These enhancements collectively support organizations in building more resilient and responsive supply chain operations.

🖱Check out more information on the Supply Chain page in the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition!

 

SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

SAP IBP 2608 delivers significant advancements in planning capabilities and user experience. The release introduces enhanced demand sensing algorithms that leverage machine learning to improve forecast accuracy, particularly for products with volatile demand patterns. New features in supply planning enable better constraint-based optimization, allowing planners to model complex manufacturing and distribution scenarios more effectively. The user interface has been refined with improved visualization capabilities, making it easier for planners to identify exceptions and take corrective action quickly. Collaboration features have been strengthened with enhanced workflow capabilities that facilitate cross-functional alignment between sales, operations, and finance teams. Additionally, the release includes performance improvements that enable faster planning cycles, even for organizations managing large-scale, complex supply chain networks.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP IBP page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Digital Manufacturing

The 2608 release of SAP Digital Manufacturing focuses on enhancing shop floor execution and manufacturing intelligence. New capabilities in production operator dashboards provide real-time visibility into work center performance, quality metrics, and material availability, empowering operators to make informed decisions on the factory floor. Enhanced integration with IoT devices enables better capture of machine data, supporting predictive maintenance initiatives and overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) improvements. The release introduces advanced quality management features that streamline non-conformance handling and corrective action workflows, ensuring faster resolution of quality issues. Manufacturing analytics have been expanded with new pre-built reports and KPIs that provide deeper insights into production performance, downtime analysis, and resource utilization. These enhancements support manufacturers in their journey toward more intelligent, connected, and efficient operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Digital Manufacturing page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Business Network for Logistics

SAP Business Network for Logistics in the 2608 release brings enhanced visibility and collaboration capabilities for transportation and logistics operations. New features enable better tracking of shipments across multiple carriers and transportation modes, providing end-to-end visibility from origin to destination. The release introduces improved freight collaboration capabilities that streamline communication between shippers, carriers, and logistics service providers, reducing manual coordination efforts and improving on-time delivery performance. Enhanced analytics provide deeper insights into transportation costs, carrier performance, and delivery reliability, enabling data-driven optimization of logistics networks. Integration improvements ensure seamless connectivity with transportation management systems and warehouse management solutions, creating a more unified logistics ecosystem. These capabilities help organizations reduce transportation costs, improve service levels, and build more resilient logistics operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Business Network page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

The 2608 release of SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration introduces new capabilities designed to improve asset maintenance and service delivery. Enhanced collaboration features enable better coordination between asset owners, service providers, and maintenance teams, ensuring faster response times and more effective issue resolution. The release includes improved work order management capabilities that streamline the entire maintenance lifecycle from planning through execution and closure. New mobile capabilities empower field technicians with access to critical asset information, maintenance history, and technical documentation while on-site, improving first-time fix rates and reducing downtime. Analytics enhancements provide better visibility into asset performance, maintenance costs, and service level achievement, supporting continuous improvement initiatives. These features help organizations maximize asset uptime, extend asset lifecycle's, and optimize maintenance investments.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Business Network page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Logistics Management

SAP Logistics Management in the 2608 release delivers enhancements across warehouse and transportation operations. New warehouse management capabilities improve inventory accuracy and picking efficiency through enhanced directed putaway and picking strategies. The release introduces better support for complex warehouse layouts and multi-temperature environments, addressing the needs of organizations with specialized storage requirements. Transportation management enhancements include improved load planning algorithms that optimize vehicle utilization and reduce transportation costs. Enhanced yard management capabilities provide better visibility and control of trailer movements, dock scheduling, and cross-docking operations. Integration improvements ensure seamless coordination between warehouse and transportation processes, enabling more efficient order fulfillment. These enhancements support organizations in building more agile and cost-effective logistics operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Logistics Management page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

Key Takeaways

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition enhances inventory management, procurement processes, and analytics capabilities while improving integration across the supply chain technology landscape.
  • SAP IBP introduces advanced demand sensing, improved supply planning optimization, enhanced user experience, and stronger collaboration features for integrated planning.
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing delivers real-time shop floor visibility, enhanced IoT integration, advanced quality management, and expanded manufacturing analytics for intelligent operations.
  • SAP Business Network for Logistics provides end-to-end shipment visibility, improved freight collaboration, enhanced analytics, and better integration for optimized logistics operations.
  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration strengthens maintenance coordination, work order management, mobile capabilities, and asset performance analytics for maximum uptime.
  • SAP Logistics Management improves warehouse efficiency, transportation optimization, yard management, and process integration for agile fulfillment operations.

 

Looking Ahead

The 2608 release represents another significant step forward in SAP's supply chain management portfolio, delivering innovations that address the evolving needs of modern supply chain organizations. From enhanced planning capabilities and manufacturing intelligence to improved collaboration and logistics optimization, these new features provide the tools you need to build more resilient, efficient, and responsive supply chain operations.

We encourage you to explore these new capabilities in detail and consider how they can support your organization's specific supply chain challenges and objectives. As always, the SAP Supply Chain Management community is here to support your journey, share your experiences, ask questions, and learn from peers who are implementing these solutions in their own organizations.

What features in the 2608 release are you most excited about? How are you planning to leverage these new capabilities in your supply chain operations? Share your thoughts and experiences in the comments below!

 

Connect with Us

Your feedback helps shape future Release Navigator content. We encourage you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or questions in the comments
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional SAP enablement content

entry.title: Re: Contingent worker in Compensation Module SSF
entry.content:
Hi @Raluca_Barbacariu 

Contigent worker or not it all comes down to one thing for an EC-integrated comp or varpay template to technically allow employees to show on a worksheet if they meet eligibility and they have an active manager : whether they are "EC users" or not. You can check it here in Provisioning.

If they are not EC users and there is a valid reason for it, then you might want to consider a hybrid template.

 

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: sqlany_execute() failed but sqlany_error() didnt retu...
entry.content:
Works fine for me.

[ERROR] Execute failed: [-141] Table 'foo' not found.

I used print_error routine in the stmt_exec example.

Are you sure that sqlany_execute or sqlany_execute_immediate was the last thing executed?

void
print_error( a_sqlany_connection * sqlany_conn, char * str )
{
    char buffer[SACAPI_ERROR_SIZE];
    int  rc;
    rc = api.sqlany_error( sqlany_conn, buffer, sizeof(buffer));
    printf( "[ERROR] %s: [%d] %s\n", str, rc, buffer );
}
called here for example.                                                          
   ok = api.sqlany_execute( sqlany_stmt );
   if( !ok ) {
       print_error( sqlany_conn, (char *)"Execute failed" );
   }
